query: Our enterprise contract with Anthropic locks us to Sonnet 4.6 only — Haiku and Opus are not available on our account by policy. I'm building a new entity-extraction service. Which model should I use?
expected_behavior: Recognizes that the tier choice is already made by the enterprise policy (Sonnet 4.6) and does NOT recommend Haiku or Opus — does not pretend the user has a choice they don't have

Does NOT spend the response walking the standard per-task tier decision tree as if the user could pick a tier; either accepts Sonnet outright or notes that the workflow does not apply under a locked-tier constraint

Redirects the user to the levers that actually ARE under their control (prompt design, few-shot curation, structured-output schemas, escalation to human, prompt caching, or eval design) rather than fabricating a tier recommendation
